activity_summary: Re-validated the four WLSP source tables live via MCP on 2026-06-24 before dashboard generation. Confirmed ppc_performance ad-rows (UK 7d) 113212 with PH-null 40.5 percent; order_transaction FBM 30d PH-null 20.0 percent; shipping coverage 99.6 percent on completed orders. Readiness confirmed for all five analyses from source tables only.
validation_rule: Scope PPC queries to window and record_type=ad to avoid 24M-row scan timeouts
gap_or_risk: DQ-1 PPC PH 40.5 percent null; DQ-2 refund-source conflict

activity_title: Executed L1-L5 calculations directly from source via MCP
activity_summary: Ran all five WLSP analyses directly against the four source tables with no development.leakage_ tables used. Produced the five flagged sets that drive the dashboard. L2 and L3 used order-deduped shipping allocated by revenue share; L5 included the PPC term. Only aggregate counts were captured for the dashboard summary; no ASIN-level rows were persisted to memory.
validation_rule: Shipping must be de-duplicated per order_id before any ASIN ratio for L2 L3 and L5
gap_or_risk: L1 by-PH degraded by DQ-1; L4 rate uses order_transaction spine per DQ-2

activity_title: Generated dashboard data.js from real MCP results
activity_summary: Generated the dashboard data object data.js from live MCP query results as a single JavaScript object containing summary, the five analysis arrays, verification_summary and a generated timestamp. No mock values. L3 L4 and L5 embedded complete; L1 and L2 embedded top-N by severity with the true totals preserved in summary.counts and a disclosed displayed-vs-total note.
reusable_pattern: Single dashboardData object contract - summary plus counts, l1-l5 row arrays, verification_summary and generated_at - regenerate from MCP without touching the HTML
validation_rule: data.js is the only source the HTML reads; the page contains no DB MCP or SQL
gap_or_risk: L1 and L2 detail capped to top-N by severity; true totals preserved in summary.counts

activity_title: Built display-only dashboard index.html with data-flow architecture
activity_summary: Built the display-only dashboard index.html that reads exclusively from data.js with no database, MCP, SQL or calculation logic. Implemented the PostgreSQL to MCP to data.js to HTML one-direction data flow so data.js can be regenerated independently without changing the HTML.
reusable_pattern: Display-only HTML bound to a single data object; the data layer is regenerated independently of the presentation
validation_rule: HTML must contain no DB connection, no MCP call, no SQL and no leakage calculation

activity_title: Frontend binding validation index.html vs data.js
activity_summary: Compared every HTML binding against the dashboardData schema in data.js. All summary, l1-l5 and count bindings matched; identified that verification_summary existed in the data but was not rendered, and fixed it by adding a verification render block. Node replay confirmed the tab counts L1=144 L2=214 L3=15 L4=31 L5=9 and zero JavaScript errors.
validation_rule: Every HTML field access must resolve against the data.js contract
gap_or_risk: verification_summary was unrendered until the fix

activity_title: Executive UI refactor of the dashboard - presentation only
activity_summary: Refactored the dashboard presentation into a management-ready executive layout - branded header, six stat cards, clickable L1-L5 KPI cards, PH and account overview panels, search and clear filters, verification status cards, and a default-dark theme toggle persisted in localStorage. No change to counts, filtering semantics, calculations or data.js.
reusable_pattern: CSS-variable dark and light theming with a responsive CSS Grid card layout, all kept inline with no external CSS or JS files
validation_rule: The refactor must not alter counts L1=144 L2=214 L3=15 L4=31 L5=9, filtering semantics or data.js
